acceptance_targets: [F017_CONTINUITY_DURATION_MISMATCH] In the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:145, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:147, a cold reader can answer all three without contradiction: how long the ring has existed, how long Marcus has been visiting, and how long Agnes has kept records. Fail if the chapter still gives incompatible month counts or still implies Agnes joined well after the ring's canonical start.

[F017_CLEANUP_CRISIS_TOO_ORDERLY] Between the revised passages beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:99 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:163, at least one beat visibly threatens exposure or completion of the cleanup, Carmen must alter the plan in response, and the reader can name what nearly went wrong and what concrete compromise or consequence followed. Fail if the sequence still reads as uninterrupted disposal with only ambient stress.

[F017_AFTERMATH_STRUCTURAL_RECURRENCE] In the revised aftermath beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:169, the scene must produce at least one new dramatic payload beyond "the cleanup is over": a disclosed truth, public risk, operational decision, or irreversible relational shift. Fail if the section can still be summarized as Carmen and Harry quietly acknowledging the end in the same register and setting as the previous chapter's debrief.

[F07_ch17_underseeded_phone] Read lines 3595-3605. (1) Carmen's discovery of the phone includes a moment of retroactive recognition — she connects the phone to something she noticed earlier. (2) The scene still creates genuine ambiguity about whether the ring is over. (3) The phone does not appear to be a device the author inserted at the last minute.
[length_chapter_17_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:145, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:147, a cold reader can answer all three without contradiction: how long the ring has existed, how long Marcus has been visiting, and how long Agnes has kept records. Fail if the chapter still gives incompatible month counts or still implies Agnes joined well after the ring's canonical start.
findings.problem: The chapter hard-codes a fourteen-month duration for Marcus's weekly visits and for Agnes's records, but the continuity sheet establishes the ring as operating for roughly eighteen months and Agnes as a co-founder. Without an explicit distinction between the ring's full lifespan and a shorter external-distribution or notebook span, the chapter contradicts canon and muddies how long Carmen's exposure has been building.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the timeline language at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:145, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:147 so one consistent chronology is legible. If the ring is eighteen months old but Marcus's visitor-log pattern and Agnes's notebooks cover only the last fourteen, state that distinction directly instead of using "fourteen months" as the chapter's default duration.

findings.acceptance_test: Between the revised passages beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:99 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:163, at least one beat visibly threatens exposure or completion of the cleanup, Carmen must alter the plan in response, and the reader can name what nearly went wrong and what concrete compromise or consequence followed. Fail if the sequence still reads as uninterrupted disposal with only ambient stress.
findings.problem: Once Harry says "We clean house," the crisis mostly becomes a sequential disposal checklist. Earl's cough and Agnes's witness add emotional pressure, but the operational cleanup itself meets almost no live interference, so a chapter whose engine is imminent exposure reads more like competent procedure than desperate improvisation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add a concrete interruption or failure point inside the cleanup engine at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:99, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:109, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:135, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:163. A plumbing problem, Devon stirring awake, a staff noise, a fight over what cannot be destroyed, or an incoming contact from Marcus would all work if they force Carmen to improvise and pay a cost while keeping Earl's cough and Agnes's grief intact.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised aftermath beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:169, the scene must produce at least one new dramatic payload beyond "the cleanup is over": a disclosed truth, public risk, operational decision, or irreversible relational shift. Fail if the section can still be summarized as Carmen and Harry quietly acknowledging the end in the same register and setting as the previous chapter's debrief.
findings.problem: After the frantic work, the chapter settles into another dim Harry/Carmen TV-room debrief that mostly certifies what the reader already knows: the ring is over, the room is empty, both are tired. Against the boundary context, this repeats the prior chapter's late-night debrief shape instead of delivering a chapter-defining new moral incision, public consequence, or irreversible choice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the post-cleanup section beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:171, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:181, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:197, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:211 so it does more than mourn closure. Either move the reckoning out of the TV room or make that scene yield a new fact, a new witness, a real split over what the ring was, or a concrete next-step lie or decision that changes the relationship before the closing phone sting.

findings.acceptance_test: Read lines 3595-3605. (1) Carmen's discovery of the phone includes a moment of retroactive recognition — she connects the phone to something she noticed earlier. (2) The scene still creates genuine ambiguity about whether the ring is over. (3) The phone does not appear to be a device the author inserted at the last minute.

evidence.excerpt: 3597: The door was mostly closed. Lakeview policy required doors to remain openable from the hall, not shut fully, but Harry had pulled it to within an inch. Through the gap, in the strip of visible room, Carmen saw him sitting in his wheelchair by the nightstand. He had something in his hand. A phone — not the prepaid, which was in pieces in the dumpster behind the building, but a different one. A cell phone she had never seen. Small, black, a flip phone like the prepaid but not the prepaid. He was holding it to his ear.

3599: He was talking. His voice was low and Carmen couldn't hear the words from the hallway — just the rhythm, the cadence of short sentences delivered with the controlled precision of his real register. Not the performance. Not the wandering mumble about Eleanor and what day was it and who were these people. The real Harry, at ten minutes to six in the morning, calling someone on a phone that Carmen didn't know existed, saying things she couldn't hear to a person she couldn't identify.

Through the gap, in the strip of visible room, Carmen saw him sitting in his wheelchair by the nightstand. He had something in his hand. 3598: 
3599: He was talking. His voice was low and Carmen couldn't hear the words from the hallway — just the rhythm, the cadence of short sentences delivered with the controlled precision of his real register. Not the performance. Not the wandering mumble about Eleanor and what day was it and who were these people. findings.problem: Harry's second hidden phone appears at lines 3597-3599 with zero prior establishment anywhere in the manuscript. Carmen sees Harry 'holding it to his ear' — 'a cell phone she had never seen. Small, black, a flip phone like the prepaid but not the prepaid.' This phone creates the novel's key unresolved question (is the ring truly over?), but it arrives as a plot device rather than a discovered detail. The reader has spent 17 chapters in this world without any hint that Harry maintains a separate communication channel. His operational paranoia and accounting-mind redundancy are established, but no physical seed for a second phone exists. The result is that the novel's most important ambiguity — whether Harry is already rebuilding — rests on a device that feels inserted for the ending rather than woven into the story.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3597-3599: KEEP the second phone scene as written — it is dramatically effective. The fix belongs in earlier chapters (see companion finding for chapter 3). However, the narration at line 3597 could be tightened to make Carmen's recognition more specific: instead of 'A phone — not the prepaid... but a different one,' give Carmen a flash of retroactive recognition — something she saw earlier but didn't register, now clicking into place. This retroactive-recognition beat only works if a seed exists in an earlier chapter. If no seed is planted elsewhere, this scene will continue to read as convenient.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
